name: Hawaiian Electric Industries, Inc.

profile.description: Hawaiian Electric Industries is a regulated electric utility serving ~95% of Hawaii's population, also operating American Savings Bank. The company faces significant litigation exposure from the 2023 Maui wildfire.

innovation.label: WEAK
innovation.analysis: Track Record (0/3): Revenue growth of 0.8% YoY with ongoing wildfire litigation challenges. AI Deployment (0/2): No AI. Market Share (2/2): Monopoly utility serving ~95% of Hawaii [1]. Pipeline (0/2): None. R&D Intensity (1/1): Renewable energy investment [1].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Utility service mission. Community (1/2): 2,659 employees serving Hawaii. Retention (0/2): No data. Insider (1/2): Some alignment [1].

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: HE shows severely challenged metrics. D/E of 186.1% is elevated [1]. ROE of 7.9%, profit margin of 4.0% both below targets. Forward P/E of 13.29 seems cheap but reflects litigation overhang. Revenue growth minimal at 0.8%. No dividend currently due to wildfire liabilities [1].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Regulated electric utility with physical infrastructure — AI cannot displace power generation and distribution [1]. AI Opportunity (0/1): None [1].

infinite_game.summary: HE is in survival mode from wildfire litigation rather than playing an infinite game.

investment_thesis: HE trades at 13.29x forward earnings as a monopoly Hawaii utility, but the Maui wildfire litigation creates existential uncertainty [1].
thesis_continued: No dividend, elevated D/E of 186.1%, and wildfire liabilities of potentially billions make this a deeply speculative situation [1].
sources: Yahoo Finance / yfinance data for HE (Hawaiian Electric), retrieved April 2026
